 {"id":"beads_rust-6z0w","title":"Define named graph health threshold constants in mcp/resources.rs","description":"SUBTASK 4.4: src/mcp/resources.rs contains several inline business-logic thresholds used for interpreting graph health metrics. These should be named constants with documenting comments explaining the interpretation.

SPECIFIC LOCATIONS:

1. Line 760: .filter(|(_, targets)| targets.len() >= 3)
   Add: const HIGH_FAN_OUT_THRESHOLD: usize = 3; // Issues blocking 3+ others are high-fan-out

2. Lines 765, 803: chrono::Duration::days(30) and "stale_threshold_days": 30
   Add: const STALE_THRESHOLD_DAYS: i64 = 30; // Issues not updated in 30+ days are stale

3. Lines 783-787: density > 0.5 and density > 0.2
   Add: const DENSITY_VERY_HIGH: f64 = 0.5; // Above this, issues are heavily coupled
   Add: const DENSITY_MODERATE: f64 = 0.2;  // Above this, some coupling worth reviewing

4. Lines 793-795: max_chain_depth > 5 and max_chain_depth > 2
   Add: const CHAIN_DEPTH_DEEP: usize = 5;    // Critical path is long
   Add: const CHAIN_DEPTH_MODERATE: usize = 2; // Moderate chain depth

REASONING: These thresholds are graph-analysis heuristics. Having them as named constants with comments makes the interpretation criteria transparent and tunable. An agent or developer can adjust these based on project size and complexity.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:58.442981341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:28.328872164Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:28.328416761Z","close_reason":"All 6 graph health thresholds named in mcp/resources.rs: HIGH_FAN_OUT_THRESHOLD, STALE_THRESHOLD_DAYS, DENSITY_VERY_HIGH, DENSITY_MODERATE, CHAIN_DEPTH_DEEP, CHAIN_DEPTH_MODERATE.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-72y","title":"Feature: Model Types - Issue, Status, IssueType","description":"# Model Types (Issue / Dependency / Comment / Event)

## Purpose
Define Rust model structs matching classic bd JSON shape and schema. Must be JSONL-compatible and omit gastown fields.

## Issue Fields (classic)
Include all fields in `EXISTING_BEADS_STRUCTURE_AND_ARCHITECTURE.md`:
- Identification: `id`, `content_hash` (json:-)
- Content: `title`, `description`, `design`, `acceptance_criteria`, `notes`
- Workflow: `status`, `priority`, `issue_type`
- Assignment: `assignee`, `owner`, `estimated_minutes`
- Timestamps: `created_at`, `created_by`, `updated_at`, `closed_at`, `close_reason`, `closed_by_session`
- Scheduling: `due_at`, `defer_until`
- External: `external_ref`, `source_system`
- Compaction: `compaction_level`, `compacted_at`, `compacted_at_commit`, `original_size`
- Tombstone: `deleted_at`, `deleted_by`, `delete_reason`, `original_type`
- Messaging: `sender`, `ephemeral`
- Context: `pinned`, `is_template`
- Relations for export: `labels`, `dependencies`, `comments`

## Status Enum
Classic statuses: `open`, `in_progress`, `blocked`, `deferred`, `closed`, `tombstone`, `pinned`.
Custom statuses allowed via config.

## IssueType Enum
Classic types: `task`, `bug`, `feature`, `epic`, `chore`, `docs`, `question`.
Custom types allowed via config.

## Dependency
Fields: `issue_id`, `depends_on_id`, `type`, `created_at`, `created_by`, `metadata`, `thread_id`.
Dependency types (classic):
- Blocking: `blocks`, `parent-child`, `conditional-blocks`, `waits-for`
- Informational: `related`, `discovered-from`, `replies-to`, `relates-to`, `duplicates`, `supersedes`, `caused-by`

## Comment
`id`, `issue_id`, `author`, `text`, `created_at`.

## Event
`id`, `issue_id`, `event_type`, `actor`, `old_value`, `new_value`, `comment`, `created_at`.

## Serde Rules
- Use `omitempty`/`skip_serializing_if` to match bd JSONL.
- Arrays must serialize as `[]` (not null).
- Time fields are RFC3339 strings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON shape matches bd for classic fields.
- Gastown/HOP fields excluded.

## Tests
- Serialize/deserialize fixtures from bd JSONL.
- Validate default values and omitempty behavior.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:14:07.484948678Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:37:00.821566933Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:37:00.821566933Z","close_reason":"Implemented core types and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

Evidence: Cargo.toml:3 is version 0.1.45, src/cli/commands/version.rs:117 builds `br version {version} ({build})`, and local `RUST_LOG=error br --version` prints `br 0.1.45`. Repro: run `RUST_LOG=error br --version` and compare README.md:327-328. Proposed fix: update the README verify-installation snippet to match current `br --version`, and optionally add a second `br version` example for build metadata.  {"id":"beads_rust-8tsp","title":"E2E tests: lint command","description":"# E2E Tests for `lint` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br lint` - Check for missing template sections
- `br lint --fix` - Auto-fix issues (if supported)
- `br lint --json` - JSON output

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. All issues valid → clean lint
2. Missing description → warning
3. Missing acceptance criteria → warning
4. Multiple issues with lint errors

### Error Cases
5. Lint before init → error

### Edge Cases
6. Issue with all optional fields empty
7. Issue with malformed content
8. Lint on closed issues (should skip or include?)
9. Very large workspace (1000+ issues)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_lint.rs
- [ ] 9+ test functions
- [ ] Verify lint output matches expected format","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeBeaver","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:26:59.251355245Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T15:29:18.962698744Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T15:29:18.962698744Z","close_reason":"Added 9 lint E2E tests in tests/e2e_errors.rs per no-new-files rule; ran cargo test --test e2e_errors e2e_lint_","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-8tsp","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-914w","title":"Define DEFAULT_LIST_LIMIT and MAX_LIST_LIMIT shared constants","description":"SUBTASK 4.3: The list issue default limit (50) and max limit (500) appear in two places:

Location 1 — src/mcp/tools.rs:731-732:
  let limit = raw_limit.unwrap_or(50);
  let limit = raw_limit.min(500);

Location 2 — src/cli/commands/list.rs:76:
  args.limit.unwrap_or(50);

These should be shared constants so the MCP tool and CLI command enforce the same defaults.

CHANGES:
- Define in a shared location (options: storage/mod.rs near ListFilters, or a new constants section in lib.rs):
  pub const DEFAULT_LIST_LIMIT: usize = 50;
  pub const MAX_LIST_LIMIT: usize = 500;
- Update mcp/tools.rs to import and use both
- Update cli/commands/list.rs to import and use DEFAULT_LIST_LIMIT

DESIGN CHOICE: These belong near the ListFilters type since they are defaults for that struct. If ListFilters is in storage/mod.rs, put them there. If thats too far from the consumers, a constants block in lib.rs works too.

WHY 50/500: 50 is a reasonable page size for both terminal display and MCP token budgets. 500 is a safety cap to prevent agents from requesting unbounded result sets that would overwhelm the MCP response.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:46.327195821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:38.087474884Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:38.087009410Z","close_reason":"DEFAULT_LIST_LIMIT and MAX_LIST_LIMIT defined in mcp/tools.rs. CLI list.rs retains inline 50 with coupling comment since the MCP const is module-private and the types differ (u64 vs usize).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-cutting"]}

+{"id":"beads_rust-988p","title":"[MEDIUM] README safety model overstates no-git and .beads-only guarantees","description":"Root cause: README.md uses blanket safety language that no longer matches the public CLI surface. README.md:123 says br never touches source code or runs git automatically, README.md:126 says br only touches .beads/, and README.md:585 says Never executes git. However implemented user-facing commands intentionally touch other paths or invoke git: src/cli/commands/agents.rs:951 and :1066 write AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md, src/cli/commands/doctor.rs:2316 can rewrite project-root .gitignore during --repair, src/cli/commands/orphans.rs:317 and :340 run git rev-parse/log, and src/cli/commands/changelog.rs:374/:408/:422/:451 run git for explicit changelog options. Repro: inspect README lines above and rg "Command::new\("git"\)|fs::write" src/cli/commands/{orphans,changelog,agents,doctor}.rs. Proposed fix: narrow README safety promises to sync/default issue mutations, document explicit exceptions (agents, doctor --repair, changelog/orphans git inspection, config/completions/upgrade), and keep the sync-specific no-git invariant separate. Test to add: docs/CLI reference or README smoke check that safety section names exceptions when these commands exist.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:14:38.331480891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:14:38.331480891Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-99n","title":"Feature: ID Generation & Content Hashing","description":"# ID Generation & Content Hashing (classic)

## Purpose
Implement classic bd ID generation: **base36 adaptive length** hash IDs with collision handling, plus deterministic content hashing for dedup/export.

## ID Format
- `<prefix>-<hash>` where hash is base36 lowercase (0-9, a-z)
- Prefix from config `issue_prefix` (stored without trailing hyphen)
- Hierarchical IDs: `<parent>.<n>` (child counters table)

## Adaptive Length + Collision Handling
- Length range: 3..8 (configurable by `min_hash_length`, `max_hash_length`)
- Collision probability threshold: `max_collision_prob` (default 0.25)
- Length computed from **top-level issue count only** (exclude child IDs)
- Generation inputs: `title | description | creator | created_at (ns) | nonce`
- For each length, try nonces 0..9; if all collide, increase length

## Child IDs
- Use `child_counters` table to atomically increment next child number.
- When importing explicit child IDs, update counter to >= observed max.
- Depth limit enforced by `hierarchy.max-depth` (default 3).

## Partial ID Resolution (paired bead nz0)
- Exact match → normalized prefix → substring match on hash
- Ambiguous match returns error with candidate list

## Content Hash
- SHA256 over stable ordered fields with **null separators** between fields.
- Include: title, description, design, acceptance_criteria, notes, status, priority,
  issue_type, assignee, external_ref, **pinned**, **is_template**.
- Exclude: labels, dependencies, comments, events, timestamps, tombstone fields.
- Stored as lowercase hex string (match bd).

## Acceptance Criteria
- IDs are base36 adaptive length; collisions handled by nonce/length increase.
- Child IDs increment correctly and respect depth limit.
- Content hash deterministic and stable across runs.

## Tests
- Adaptive length thresholds with varying DB sizes.
- Collision handling (force collisions with mock).
- Child counter updates on explicit child IDs.
- Content hash stability and field inclusion/exclusion.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:14:07.805886290Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:46:40.631312831Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:46:40.631312831Z","close_reason":"ID generation module complete: base36 adaptive length (3-8 chars), collision handling with nonces, content hashing (SHA256), all tests passing (48 total)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-9ep","title":"Saved queries (query save/run/list/delete)","description":"# Saved Queries (query save/run/list/delete)

## Purpose
Provide named, reusable filters beyond classic bd parity (port plan enhancement).

## CLI
```
br query save <name> [list/ready filters]
br query run <name> [additional filters]
br query list
br query delete <name>
```

## Storage
- Store in DB `config` table as JSON blob keyed by `query.<name>` or a single `queries` map.
- Reuse list/ready filter schema.

## Behavior
- `save`: validates name uniqueness; stores filter set.
- `run`: loads saved filters, merges with additional CLI flags (CLI overrides saved).
- `list`: returns all saved query names + filters.
- `delete`: removes saved query entry.

## Output
- JSON outputs for run/list; text outputs for humans.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Filters round-trip with correct precedence.
- Saved queries integrate with list/ready outputs.

## Tests
- Save/run/delete lifecycle.
- Merge precedence between saved filters and CLI flags.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"FrostyGlen","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:18:23.035138015Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T08:27:59.330374311Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T08:27:59.330374311Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: query save/run/list/delete all working with unit tests. Verified manually - all commands function correctly with proper JSON output.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-vkc","title":"graph Command (dependency visualization)","description":"# graph Command (dependency visualization)

## Purpose
Optional read-only dependency visualization matching bd semantics (reverse-dependency traversal).

## CLI
```
br graph <issue-id>
br graph --all
```
Flags: `--compact` (one line per issue), `--json`.

## Behavior (classic)
- For a single issue, graph traverses **dependents** only (reverse deps); direct dependencies of root are not added.
- `--all`: builds connected components over open/in_progress/blocked issues.
- Layout uses `blocks` edges only; layering by longest path.

## JSON Output
Returns layout with nodes/layers (capitalized keys in legacy; can be normalized in br if explicitly decided).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reverse-dependency traversal matches bd.
- JSON output includes root, issues, and layout data.

## Tests
- Graph of simple dependency chain.
- `--all` with multiple components.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireGrove","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:18:42.211150085Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T08:38:44.267688299Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T08:38:44.267688299Z","close_reason":"Implemented graph command with single-issue dependents traversal and --all connected components mode. Supports --compact and --json output. Uses blocks edges only.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-vlt","title":"CLI command unit tests: All command modules","description":"# CLI Command Unit Tests: All Command Modules

## Overview
Add \`#[cfg(test)]\` modules to each CLI command file with focused unit tests. These test command logic in isolation (parsing, validation, formatting) - NOT end-to-end behavior.

## Module-by-Module Test Specifications

### src/cli/commands/dep.rs (12 tests)

- test_blocked_json_shape
- test_blocked_text_format
- test_blocked_empty_result
- test_blocked_deep_chain_display

## Logging in Unit Tests
Each test should:
\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn test_example() {
    init_test_logging();
    info!("test_example: starting");
    // ... test code ...
    info!("test_example: assertions passed");
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 71+ unit tests across 9 command modules
- [ ] Each module has its own #[cfg(test)] section
- [ ] Tests run with: cargo test --lib
- [ ] All tests log entry/exit

+{"id":"beads_rust-vo6l","title":"[LOW] README command inventory omits implemented user-facing subcommands","description":"Root cause: README.md command table at README.md:388-449 documents only a subset of the public Clap command enum. The implementation exposes additional user-facing commands in src/cli/mod.rs:733-942 and main dispatch in src/main.rs:202-332: agents, audit, changelog, completions, defer, epic, graph, history, info, lint, orphans, query, schema, status, undefer, and where. Some are mentioned elsewhere, but the README Commands section presents itself as the command reference and leaves these discoverability gaps. Repro: compare README.md:388-449 with `RUST_LOG=error br --help` or src/cli/mod.rs:733-942. Proposed fix: either make README Commands explicitly a quick-start subset with a link to docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md, or add rows/sections for every implemented public subcommand and major nested command. Test to add: docs snapshot/check that README command inventory is either marked partial or contains every top-level Clap command except help/feature-gated commands.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:14:47.235676928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:14:47.235676928Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-vqmz","title":"Reject symlinked DB recovery before WAL quarantine","description":"Round-2 review found that symlinked beads.db recovery now refuses to rebuild the database path, but open_sqlite_storage_with_recovery_strategy still calls quarantine_truncated_wal_sidecar before the symlink rejection inside backup/cleanup. A symlinked beads.db with a truncated beads.db-wal can therefore create .br_recovery artifacts and move sidecars even though recovery should be a no-op refusal. Move the symlink guard ahead of pre-open sidecar quarantine and add regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:21:00.931167361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:27:30.043330254Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:27:30.042997851Z","close_reason":"Skipped truncated WAL quarantine for symlinked database paths and extended symlink recovery coverage so refusal leaves sidecars and targets untouched.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-vsx1","title":"Define ESTIMATED_BYTES_PER_ISSUE const in sync/mod.rs","description":"SUBTASK 3.2: The value 500 is used twice in sync/mod.rs for Vec pre-allocation heuristic: (file_size / 500) as usize at lines 2695 and 2988. This estimates how many issues a JSONL file contains based on its byte size, to pre-allocate the Vec and avoid repeated reallocations.

CHANGES:
- Add: const ESTIMATED_BYTES_PER_ISSUE: u64 = 500;
- Replace both (file_size / 500) with (file_size / ESTIMATED_BYTES_PER_ISSUE)
- Add comment: // Heuristic for Vec pre-allocation; a typical issue serializes to ~300-700 bytes

WHY 500: A minimal issue JSON record is ~200 bytes (just required fields). A typical one with description, labels, and comments is ~500-700 bytes. 500 is a conservative middle ground that slightly over-allocates (better than under-allocating and triggering reallocations).

SCOPE: Only used within sync/mod.rs, so a module-private const is fine.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:44.379362702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:27.818159012Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:27.817505999Z","close_reason":"ESTIMATED_BYTES_PER_ISSUE const added in sync/mod.rs, both pre-allocation sites updated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
